subtitle: A pair of coves under a pointed sea stack south of Half Moon Bay, a public beach for a century — until the billionaire Vinod Khosla bought it and locked the only gate, triggering the landmark fight over whether anyone can privatize California's public coast.
spike_statement: A century-old public beach south of Half Moon Bay that a billionaire bought and locked shut in 2008 — the landmark test of whether money can privatize California's coast.

levers.photo_tone: a cool grey-blue Northern California cove under a sharp pointed sea stack, fog on the headlands, cold Pacific surf, and above it all a locked gate on the only road down
